In that table, if the first player picks fight and the second picks sharing, the first player gets more than the second, and if the second also picks fight the first still does not get less than the sharing way. From those numbers they think the first idea follows: the fight way is a plan that cannot be pushed out by any other plan, so it must be the plan that stays when the game is played many times.

The second idea they think follows is that because the fight way wins against any other way in the table, every player should end up using the fight way and the game will stay at that point. They also think that because the fight way does not lose any what you get in the table, no other plan can give a more good what you get, so the fight way must be the most good in all.

The thinking behind these ideas is not completely good. The table also shows that when both players use the fight way they each get a lower how much than if they both used the sharing way. That means there is a chance for a different plan – the sharing way – to give both players more if most players are using fight. In that situation a player who changes to sharing can get more, so the fight way can be pushed out. Also the reasoning looks only at the two ways and does not consider ways that mix the two, which the table can also show. Because of those missing points, the idea that the fight way is a plan that stays the same in the share game is not completely good. The first idea about the numbers is true, but the step that says those numbers make the fight way the only safe plan does not hold up when all view from the table is considered.

english_query:
Write a clear explanation of the following idea. Think step by step. Make sure someone who has never studied this before could understand. Write in plain running prose -- no asterisks, no bold, no bullet lists, no numbered headers.

Topic: Think about each column separately: when your rival picks Sword, which of your two numbers is larger? Now do the same when they pick Magic. The larger number in each case tells you the dominating strategy.

Explanation:
ugf_response: Imagine you are in a very simple game with another person. In this game each of you can only pick one of two moves. Call one move the stick and call the other move the strange force. Both you and the other person write down a number of points you would win or lose for each possible pair of moves. This makes a small paper. The lines across the paper show your two moves, the lines down the paper show the two moves of the other person, and each little box in the paper shows the number of points you would get if that pair of moves is played. Now you want to pick a move. One way to think is to look at one line down at a time, because each line down is a single choice the other person could make. First, think that the other person has already picked the stick. In that line down you see two numbers: the number you would get if you answer with the stick, and the number you would get if you answer with the strange force. Look at those two numbers. If the number in the stick line across is more, then the stick is the better answer when the other person picks the stick. If the number in the strange force line across is more, then the strange force is the better answer in that case. In other words, the more number in the "other picks stick" line down tells you which of your own moves wins more when the other picks stick. Do the same for the other line down, the one that goes with the other person picking the strange force. Look at the two numbers in that line down: the number you would get by playing the stick against strange force, and the number you would get by playing strange force against strange force. The more of those two numbers shows the move that gives you more points when the other picks strange force. When you have done this for both lines down, you have found for each possible choice of the other person which of your two moves gives the more points. If the same move – like the stick – has the more number in both lines down, then that move is a move that is best for you no matter what the other does. If the more number in the stick line down belongs to the stick but the more number in the strange force line down belongs to the strange force, then no single move is best for all cases; your best pick is made from what you think the other will pick. So by looking at each line down alone and always picking the more number in that line down, you can see in one look which of your two choices is the best for all cases, if any. This step by step line down look turns the idea of "best for all cases" into a simple picture that any person can follow, even if you do not know about game talk.

Explanation:
ugf_response: Think you look at a choice that can end in many different the things that happen. Each the thing that happens has a number that tells how much you will win or the thing you give away – that number is the win or the thing you give away number. At the same time each the thing that happens is not sure; there is a chance that it will happen, and that chance is a number from the number that means nothing to one. To see how good the whole choice is, you do not look at one win or the thing you give away number alone; you put together the numbers with their chance numbers. You do this by times each win or the thing you give away number by its chance number. This times tells you how much that the thing that happens adds, most of the time, to the whole. After you have times each win or the thing you give away number by its chance number, you add all those new numbers. The whole number you get is the most of the time win or the thing you give away number of the choice. If you use a feel way about money or other things, you put new each win or the thing you give away with its feel number and do the same times and add to get the most of the time feel number, called the most of the time feel. Now use this idea for the choice we call the bright light fire. First, write down every the thing that happens that could happen when you press the bright light thing you press. Maybe there are three: the light hits the thing you point at and you win 100 points, the light does not hit and you win the number that means nothing, and the light turns on you and you give away 50 points. Next, you need the chance numbers for each the thing that happens. Maybe the hit has a chance number of 0.6, the miss 0.3, and the turn on you 0.1. Now use the feel number of each win or the thing you give away – if you use a simple way, the feel number of 100 is 100, of the number that means nothing is the number that means nothing, and of -50 is -50. Times each feel number by its chance number: 0.6 times 100 is 60; 0.3 times the number that means nothing is the number that means nothing; 0.1 times -50 is -5. Then add those three new numbers: 60 and the number that means nothing and the thing you give away 5 is 55. That number, 55, is the most of the time feel number of the bright light fire. To see if the bright light fire is a good choice, do the same steps for the other things you think about, like the hot air thing or the thing that makes you more safe. Maybe the hot air thing has a chance 0.4 of giving 150 points, a chance 0.5 of giving 20 points, and a chance 0.1 of giving away 30 points. Times and add gives 0.4 times 150 is 60, and 0.5 times 20 is 10, and 0.1 times -30 is -3, the whole number most of the time feel number is 67. If the thing that makes you more safe has a chance 0.8 of giving 40 points and a chance 0.2 of giving the number that means nothing, its most of the time feel number is 0.8 times 40 and 0.2 times the number that means nothing, which is 32. Now you look at the three most of the time feel numbers: the bright light fire gives 55, the hot air thing gives 67, and the thing that makes you more safe gives 32. The biggest most of the time feel number belongs to the hot air thing, which means that, most of the time, picking the hot air thing would give you the most win as the chance numbers you used. The bright light fire is better than the thing that makes you more safe but not as good as the hot air thing. This look tells you which thing you should like more if you trust the chance numbers and the feel way you use. The whole way – times each win or the thing you give away number by its chance number and then add – is the main part of chance weighting and most of the time feel work.
